# CTOS Portfolio
# 14 — Crab Eyes Favicon System

## Vision

The favicon should evolve from:

Animated Brand Mark

into:

Living Identity System

The favicon should become a miniature representation of the CTOS Sentinel Crab.

Not a full crab.

Not a simplified logo.

But a recognizable pair of sentinel eyes.

---

# Strategic Goal

When a user has 30 browser tabs open.

The CTOS tab should remain recognizable instantly.

Users should identify:

CTOS

from:

Two Eyes.

---

# Why Not Full Crab

Browser favicons commonly render at:

16x16
32x32
48x48

A complete crab contains:

- eyes
- shell
- claws
- legs
- antenna

At favicon size:

Most details disappear.

Visual noise increases.

Recognition decreases.

---

# Core Identity

## Sentinel Eyes

The favicon becomes:

CTOS Sentinel Eyes

---

Visual Characteristics

- Symmetrical
- Minimal
- Observant
- Slightly robotic
- Slightly organic

---

Design Keywords

Observe

Monitor

Infrastructure

Systems

Awareness

---

# Favicon Architecture

Layer 1

Background Shape

Optional.

---

Layer 2

Left Eye

---

Layer 3

Right Eye

---

Layer 4

Pupils

---

Layer 5

Signal Ring

Optional.

---

# State System

The favicon becomes state driven.

---

State 01

Default

Purpose:

Normal browsing.

Behavior:

Eyes open.

No motion.

---

State 02

Blink

Purpose:

Show life.

Behavior:

Random blink.

Frequency:

15-45 seconds.

Randomized.

Never predictable.

---

State 03

Observe Left

Purpose:

Awareness.

Behavior:

Pupils move left.

Pause.

Return.

---

State 04

Observe Right

Purpose:

Awareness.

Behavior:

Pupils move right.

Pause.

Return.

---

State 05

Focus

Purpose:

Deep attention.

Behavior:

Eyes narrow slightly.

Pupils center.

---

State 06

Sleep

Purpose:

Inactive state.

Behavior:

Eyes closed.

Used when:

- hidden tab
- inactive session

---

# Animation Timing System

Rule:

Avoid loops.

---

Bad

setInterval(5000)

---

Good

Random timing windows.

Examples:

Blink:

15-45s

Observe:

20-60s

Micro movement:

10-25s

---

# Behavior Philosophy

The favicon should never appear busy.

It should feel:

Patient.

Watching.

Waiting.

---

# Browser Context Awareness

## Active Tab

Eyes open.

---

## Background Tab

Reduced activity.

---

## Long Hidden Session

Sleep mode.

---

## Return To Tab

Wake-up animation.

Sequence:

Closed
↓
Half Open
↓
Open
↓
Focus

---

# Integration With Crab System

The favicon should share the same behavior language as the CTOS Crab.

---

Crab Idle
↕
Favicon Blink

---

Crab Observe
↕
Favicon Eye Tracking

---

Crab Sleep
↕
Favicon Sleep

---

# Future Notification System

Phase 2

Optional.

---

Notification State

Signal ring pulse.

---

New Visitor Milestone

Subtle glow.

---

System Discovery

Short signal flash.

---

# SVG Implementation Strategy

Use:

favicon.svg

---

Requirements:

- Pure SVG
- Minimal paths
- Minimal DOM nodes
- Crisp at 16px
- Crisp at 32px

---

Target SVG Complexity

Less than 4KB.

---

# Accessibility

Respect reduced motion preferences.

If motion is disabled:

Static eyes.

No blinking.

---

# Performance Budget

Target Cost:

Near Zero.

---

Avoid:

Heavy scripting.

Continuous animation loops.

Large SVG filters.

---

# Success Criteria

Users recognize CTOS from favicon alone.

---

Users notice subtle life-like behavior.

---

Users do not perceive the favicon as distracting.

---

The favicon feels connected to the CTOS Crab ecosystem.

---

# Final Vision

The favicon becomes the smallest living component inside the CTOS ecosystem.

A pair of sentinel eyes quietly observing.

Simple enough to be recognized instantly.

Alive enough to be remembered.
